metadata.title: Guideline on Similar Biological Medicinal Products
metadata.notes: No prior extraction found in output-v2, v3, or v4. Full extraction performed.

rules.rule_text: Comparability studies are required to generate evidence substantiating the similar nature, in terms of quality, safety and efficacy, of the new similar biological medicinal product and the chosen reference medicinal product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comparability studies are needed to generate evidence substantiating the similar nature, in terms of quality, safety and efficacy, of the new similar biological medicinal product and the chosen reference medicinal product authorised in the Community.
cross_references: Section 4, Part II, Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC

rules.rule_text: The generic approach (bioequivalence by bioavailability studies) is not appropriate for biological/biotechnology-derived products; the similar biological medicinal products approach based on a comparability exercise must be followed.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - Application of 'Similar Biological Medicinal Products' Approach
rules.verbatim_quote: Due to the complexity of biological/biotechnology-derived products the generic approach is scientifically not appropriate for these products. The 'similar biological medicinal products' approach, based on a comparability exercise, will then have to be followed.
rules.exceptions: Standard generic approach applies only to chemically derived medicinal products

rules.rule_text: The similar biological medicinal product shall, with regard to the quality data, fulfill all requirements for Module 3 as defined in Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC and satisfy the technical requirements of the monographs of the European Pharmacopoeia and any additional requirements defined in relevant CHMP and ICH guidelines.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - Application of 'Similar Biological Medicinal Products' Approach
rules.verbatim_quote: The similar biological medicinal product shall, with regard to the quality data, fulfill all requirements for Module 3 as defined in Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC and satisfy the technical requirements of the monographs of the European Pharmacopoeia and any additional requirements, such as defined in relevant CHMP and ICH guidelines

rules.rule_text: The requirements to demonstrate safety and efficacy of similar biological medicinal products must comply with the data requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - Application of 'Similar Biological Medicinal Products' Approach
rules.verbatim_quote: The requirements to demonstrate safety and efficacy of similar biological medicinal products have to comply with the data requirements laid down in Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC.
rules.exceptions: For situations where product-class specific guidance is not available, applicants are encouraged to seek scientific advice from EU Regulatory Authorities

rules.rule_text: In order to support pharmacovigilance monitoring, the specific medicinal product given to the patient must be clearly identified, since subtle differences may exist between similar biological medicinal products from different manufacturers.
rules.source_section: Section 2.1 - Application of 'Similar Biological Medicinal Products' Approach
rules.verbatim_quote: Therefore, in order to support pharmacovigilance monitoring, the specific medicinal product given to the patient should be clearly identified.

rules.rule_text: The chosen reference medicinal product must be a medicinal product authorised in the Community on the basis of a complete dossier in accordance with Article 8 of Directive 2001/83/EC.
rules.verbatim_quote: The chosen reference medicinal product must be a medicinal product authorised in the Community, on the basis of a complete dossier in accordance with the provisions of Article 8 of Directive 2001/83/EC, as amended.

rules.rule_text: The same reference medicinal product must be used throughout the entire comparability program for quality, safety and efficacy studies to allow generation of coherent data and conclusions.
rules.verbatim_quote: The chosen reference medicinal product, defined on the basis of its marketing authorisation in the Community, should be used throughout the comparability program for quality, safety and efficacy studies during the development of a similar biological medicinal product in order to allow the generation of coherent data and conclusions.

rules.rule_text: Data generated from comparability studies with medicinal products authorised outside the Community may only provide supportive information and cannot serve as primary comparability evidence.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data generated from comparability studies with medicinal products authorised outside the Community may only provide supportive information.

rules.rule_text: The active substance of a similar biological medicinal product must be similar, in molecular and biological terms, to the active substance of the reference medicinal product; different molecular variants cannot be cross-referenced.
rules.verbatim_quote: The active substance of a similar biological medicinal product must be similar, in molecular and biological terms, to the active substance of the reference medicinal product. For example, a medicinal product containing interferon alfa-2a manufactured by Company X claiming to be similar to another biological medicinal product should refer to a reference medicinal product containing as its active substance interferon alfa-2a. Therefore, a medicinal product containing interferon alfa-2b could not be considered as the reference medicinal product.

rules.rule_text: The pharmaceutical form, strength, and route of administration of the similar biological medicinal product should be the same as the reference product; any differences must be justified by additional data and appropriate studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: The pharmaceutical form, strength and route of administration of the similar biological medicinal product should be the same as that of the reference medicinal product. When the pharmaceutical form, the strength or the route of administration is not the same; additional data in the context of the comparability exercise should be provided. Any differences between the similar biological medicinal product and the reference medicinal product will have to be justified by appropriate studies on a case-by-case basis.
rules.exceptions: Case-by-case justification accepted for differences in pharmaceutical form, strength, or route

rules.rule_text: Vaccines cannot submit a reduced dossier claiming similarity; they must be considered on a case-by-case basis and applicants should seek advice from EU Regulatory Authorities.
rules.source_section: Section 3.3 - Immunologicals such as Vaccines and Allergens
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccines are complex biological medicinal products. Currently, it seems unlikely that these products may be thoroughly characterised at a molecular level. Consequently, vaccines have to be considered on a case-by-case basis. Applicants should take appropriate advice from the EU Regulatory Authorities.

rules.rule_text: For blood or plasma-derived products, it is not acceptable to submit a reduced clinical dossier when claiming similarity; applications must still satisfy full safety and efficacy requirements for 'new products'.
rules.source_section: Section 3.4 - Blood or Plasma-Derived Products and their Recombinant Alternatives
rules.verbatim_quote: In view of the complex and variable physico-chemical, biological and functional characteristics of the products listed in the BPWG guidelines mentioned below, it will not be acceptable to submit a reduced clinical dossier when claiming similarity to a reference medicinal product. As a result, applications for such similar products will still need to satisfy the safety and efficacy requirements described in these BPWG guidelines for 'new products'.

rules.rule_text: CHMP guidelines addressing comparability studies must always be read in conjunction with relevant legislative and administrative provisions in force in the EU.
rules.verbatim_quote: The CHMP guidelines addressing the planning and conduct of comparability studies should always be read in conjunction with relevant legislative and administrative provisions in force in the EU.
